title: tragacantha berteroana
intro: Tragacantha berteroana, also known as Chilean milk thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the Silybum genus and is native to the Andes Mountains in South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in relation to its silymarin content, which has been shown to have liver-protective and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tragacantha berteroana include the potential to reduce liver damage and inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a supplement or tincture, and can be found in various forms, including capsules, teas, and extracts.
